children.title: SpaceX Preferred and Common Stock
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/SpaceX Preferred and Common Stock
children.summary: The Company has five stock classes: four common (Class A, B, C, D) and one preferred (SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock). As of March 31, 2026, authorized common shares total 54,657 million, with specific allocations per class, and preferred shares total 2,607 million. In May 2026, a 5-for-1 stock split was approved for Class A, B, and C common stock.

children.title: xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Common Stock
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Common Stock
children.summary: On March 28, 2025, xAI adopted an Amended and Restated Articles of Incorporation, which established its capital structure and designated multiple classes of common stock and several series of redeemable convertible preferred stock. The Articles were subsequently amended and restated through January 30, 2026 (collectively, the “xAI Articles of Incorporation”) to add and authorize additional series of redeemable convertible preferred stock with no economic changes to any previously existing series. Pursuant to the xAI Articles of Incorporation, xAI's authorized capitalization prior to the xAI Merger consisted of three classes of common, stock, which are designated Class A common stock (“xAI Class A”), Class B common stock (“xAI Class B”), Limited Voting common stock (“xAI Limited Voting”), (collectively the “xAI Common Stock”) and several series of redeemable convertible preferred stock (collectively the “xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock”). All references to “xAI Class” refer to that particular class of xAI Common Stock and all references to “xAI Series” refer to that particular series of xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock.

children.title: Effect of the xAI Merger
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger
children.summary: The text describes the conversion of xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock into SpaceX Common Stock upon the xAI Merger Date. Each share of xAI Series A-1, B, C, D, and E preferred stock (Low Vote Stock) converted into 0.1433 shares of SpaceX Class A Common Stock, while Series A (High Vote Stock) converted into 0.1433 shares of SpaceX Class B Common Stock, with fractional shares rounded up. Eligible service providers holding Series A could elect cash of $75.46 per share. All converted shares were canceled and retired. The conversion is recognized only at the merger closing, not retrospectively, so historical financial statements reflect the preferred stock as outstanding prior to the merger.

children.title: xAI Warrants
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/xAI Warrants
children.summary: xAI also issued warrants to customers that were outstanding as of the effective date of the xAI Merger, which had a ten-year term originally set to expire in 2035, with an exercise price equal to the par value of the stock, and vesting terms that resulted in the warrants vesting proportionally to the payments received under the related agreement. The closing of the xAI Merger triggered an acceleration clause in which all outstanding xAI warrants, both vested and unvested components, were automatically exercised on a cashless basis exercised and converted into fully vested SpaceX Class A Common Stock at the exchange ratio of 0.1433 (on a pre-2026 Stock Split basis).

children.title: xAI and X Common Stock
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/xAI and X Common Stock
children.summary: Upon the effective date of the xAI Merger, every outstanding share of xAI Common Stock, whether Class A, Class B, or Limited Voting, converted into the right to receive SpaceX Common Stock at a fixed exchange ratio of 0.1433 SpaceX shares per share of xAI Common Stock, unless the holder was an eligible service provider and elected to receive cash of \$75.46 per share of xAI Class A or Class B (on a pre-2026 Stock Split basis). No fractional SpaceX shares were issued and all share amounts were rounded up to the nearest whole number. Any shares of xAI Common Stock previously held by the Company were canceled and retired and did not receive any consideration.

children.title: Effect of the X Merger
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Effect of the X Merger
children.summary: Upon the effective date of the X Merger, each class of common stock of X Holdings Corp. (“X Common Stock”) was converted to 2.776 shares of xAI Common Stock of the same class (rounded down to the nearest whole share), each class of common stock of X.AI Corp. (“xAI Corp. Common Stock”) was converted to 1.000 share of xAI Common Stock of the same class, and each series of X.AI Corp. preferred stock (“xAI Corp. Preferred Stock”) (other than shares held by X or any of its subsidiaries) was converted to 1.000 share of xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock of the same series. As a result of the Mergers, all of X, X.AI Corp. and xAI Common Stock are being presented in the historical financial statements as if they had been converted into SpaceX Common Stock at the applicable exchange rate for all periods presented through the date of the xAI Merger. As such, all shares of historical X, X.AI Corp. and xAI Common Stock are included in the share counts for SpaceX Common Stock below. X.AI Corp. and xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock are being presented in the consolidated financial statements at historical values with an adjustment to the conversion rate at the applicable exchange ratio per the xAI Merger.

children.title: Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock
children.summary: Information for each series of SpaceX and xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock (collectively, the “Combined Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock”) is as follows: [tables/table-140 Preferred Stock.html] (1) The number of issued redeemable convertible preferred stock is equal to the number of outstanding redeemable convertible preferred stock, with the exception of xAI Series A and xAI Series D, of which the number of issued shares is 1,000.0 million and 175.0 million as of December 31, 2025, respectively, due to redeemable convertible preferred stock held by X and SpaceX, respectively. The following describes the various rights and preferences of the SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock:

children.title: Dividend Provisions
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock/Dividend Provisions
children.summary: On a per annum basis, holders of shares of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock are entitled to receive dividends prior and in preference to any declaration or payment of any dividend to common shareholders at a rate described in the table above for each outstanding share of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock. Any such dividends are declared at the discretion of the Board of Directors and are not cumulative. For the period from inception through March 31, 2026, no dividends on SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock have been declared. The SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock do not participate in distributions beyond their preferred dividend as described above.

children.title: Liquidation Preference
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock/Liquidation Preference
children.summary: The text describes the ranking and liquidation preferences of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock series. Series are issued chronologically and alphabetically, with later series senior to earlier ones, except for specific parity groups: Series A, A-1, B, B-1, and C are on parity and junior to later series; Series E, F, and G are on parity, senior to earlier series, and junior to later ones. In liquidation, holders receive the greater of their liquidation preference plus unpaid dividends, or the amount from conversion to common stock. Remaining assets after distributions go to common stockholders on a pro rata basis.

children.title: Conversion Rights
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock/Conversion Rights
children.summary: SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock (Series A and B) can be voluntarily converted into Class A, B, or C Common Stock, while other series convert into Class A or C. Conversion rate is initial price divided by conversion price; each share converts into 50 common shares after the 2026 Stock Split. Automatic conversion occurs upon a Qualified IPO (market cap ≥$6B, proceeds ≥$250M) or by holder consent. Transfers of Series A or B (except Permitted Transfers) trigger automatic cancellation and conversion into Series A-1 or B-1.

children.title: Voting Rights
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock/Voting Rights
children.summary: Holders of each share of Series A and Series B have the right to ten votes for each share of Class B into which such share is convertible. Holders of each share of all other series of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock have the right to one vote for each share of Class A into which such share is convertible. Such holders will have full voting rights and powers equal to the voting rights and powers of the holders of SpaceX Common Stock, except as required by law.

children.title: Classification
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock/Classification
children.summary: The liquidation preference provisions of the SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock are considered contingent redemption provisions as deemed liquidation events such as a change of control are not solely within the control of the Company. Accordingly, SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock are presented outside of permanent equity on the Company's consolidated balance sheets as Redeemable convertible preferred stock. SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock has not been remeasured to their redemption amount as they are not currently redeemable or probable of becoming redeemable.

children.title: Common Stock
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Common Stock
children.summary: The following describes all of the activity that occurred within each class of SpaceX Common Stock during the three months ended March 31, 2026 and 2025, incorporating all activity that occurred within the class of xAI Common Stock on an as-converted basis to the class of SpaceX Common Stock it was converted into per the xAI Merger and X Merger. [tables/table-141 Stock Changes.html] The following describes the various rights and preferences of the SpaceX Common Stock:

children.title: Dividend Provisions
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Common Stock/Dividend Provisions
children.summary: Subject to the prior rights of holders of all classes and series of stock at the time outstanding having prior rights as to dividends, holders of SpaceX Common Stock shall be entitled to receive, when, as and if declared by the Board of Directors, out of any funds legally available, such dividends as may be declared from time to time by the Board of Directors. For the period from inception through March 31, 2026, no dividends were declared on SpaceX Common Stock.

children.title: Liquidation Rights
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Common Stock/Liquidation Rights
children.summary: In the event of a liquidation, dissolution, or winding up of the Company, upon the completion of the distributions required with respect to the SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock, if assets remain in the Company, the then remaining assets will be distributed on an equal priority, pro rata basis to the holders of SpaceX Common Stock.

children.title: Conversion Rights
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Common Stock/Conversion Rights
children.summary: Each share of Class B is convertible at the option of the holder, at any time, into one share of Class A. Each share of Class B will automatically convert into one share of Class A upon a transfer, other than a Permitted Transfer (as defined in the charter), of such share of Class B.

children.title: Voting Rights
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Common Stock/Voting Rights
children.summary: Each holder of Class A is entitled to one vote for each share held. Each holder of Class B is entitled to ten votes for each share held. The holders of Class C have no voting rights, except as required by law. Voting rights with respect to Class D will be established when and if any shares of Class D are issued by the Board of Directors.

children.title: Reserve for Unissued Shares of Common Stock
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Effect of the xAI Merger/Reserve for Unissued Shares of Common Stock
children.summary: The Company is required to reserve and keep available out of its authorized but unissued shares of SpaceX Common Stock such number of shares sufficient to effect the conversion of all outstanding shares of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Class B, as applicable, plus shares granted and available for grant under the Company's share plans. The amount of such shares of the SpaceX Common Stock reserved for these purposes at March 31, 2026 is as follows: Number of Shares [tables/table-142 Stock Data Table.html]

children.title: Share Repurchases
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity/Share Repurchases
children.summary: During the three months ended March 31, 2026, the Company repurchased \$2,413 million or 25.4 million shares of SpaceX Common Stock from eligible current and former xAI employees as part of the xAI Merger. During the three months ended March 31, 2026, the Company also repurchased of 30.5 million shares of SpaceX Common Stock and 2.1 million shares of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock for \$1,933 million in a number of unrelated transactions with existing shareholders at their then-current fair market value. Similarly, the Company repurchased \$508 million or 28.0 million shares of SpaceX Common Stock from eligible current and former employees and existing shareholders during the three ended March 31, 2025. The Company only repurchased shares held by eligible participants for more than six months at a purchase price per share equal to the then current fair market value. All SpaceX shares repurchased to date have been retired.

children.title: Note 13 - Earnings per Share
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 13 - Earnings per Share
children.summary: The following table presents the reconciliation of net loss attributable to common shareholders to net loss used in computing basic and diluted net income loss per share of common stock: [tables/table-143 Loss Per Share.html] (1) The excess of fair market value over the consideration transferred for the repurchase of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock was treated as a deemed dividend and resulted in an increase to net loss attributable to common shareholders in the calculation of loss per share. The following potentially dilutive securities on an as-converted basis are excluded from the calculation of diluted net loss per share attributable to common shareholders for the periods presented because the impact of including them would be anti-dilutive (refer to Note 14, Share-based Compensation for additional details): [tables/table-144 Financial Data.html] The table above excludes 1,319.1 million and 14.3 million share-based compensation awards outstanding as of March 31, 2026 and 2025, respectively, as these awards are subject to performance and market conditions that were not met as of those dates.

children.title: Note 14 - Share-based Compensation
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 14 - Share-based Compensation

children.title: X and xAI Mergers
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 14 - Share-based Compensation/X and xAI Mergers
children.summary: As part of the xAI Merger, each xAI option for a share of xAI common stock outstanding and unexercised at the time of the xAI Merger (vested and unvested) was converted into a SpaceX option to receive 0.1433 shares of SpaceX Class A or Class B Common Stock (on a pre-2026 Stock Split basis), as applicable, under the same terms and conditions (including the vesting and exercisability conditions) as the original xAI stock options at an exercise price equal to the original xAI option exercise price divided by 0.1433. Each xAI RSU that was vested and outstanding was converted to the right to receive 0.1433 of a share of SpaceX Class A or Class B Common Stock (on a pre-2026 Stock Split basis), as applicable. Each xAI RSU that was unvested was converted to 0.1433 of a SpaceX RSU. Each xAI RSA was converted to 0.1433 shares of SpaceX RSA for SpaceX Class A or Class B Common Stock (on a pre-2026 Stock Split basis), as applicable, with the same terms and conditions (including the vesting terms). Refer to Note 12, Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity for additional details. As part of the X Merger, each X RSU that was outstanding was converted to a xAI RSU to receive 2.776 shares of xAI Common Stock.

children.title: General
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 14 - Share-based Compensation/General
children.summary: The Company grants RSUs, RSAs, and non-statutory options to eligible employees, key executives, and certain non-employee service providers (collectively, the “Plans”). The Company also has a number of performance-based awards. The Company offers an ESPP, under which eligible employees can purchase the Company’s Common Stock at a discounted price. The Company also offers a Non-Qualified Employee Stock Purchase Plan (“NQ ESPP”), under which employees can purchase the Company’s Common Stock at the fair market value. In April 2026, the Company cancelled the NQ ESPP.

children.title: Officer Equity Awards
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 14 - Share-based Compensation/Officer Equity Awards
children.summary: In January 2026, the Company granted Elon Musk 1,000 million performance-based restricted shares (SpaceX CEO Award) vesting upon market cap milestones from $500B to $7.5T and a Mars colony. In March 2026, it replaced the xAI Award with 302.1 million shares (AI CEO Award) vesting on market cap milestones from $1.065T to $6.565T and non-Earth data centers. The CFO Award was amended to vest based on adjusted EBITDA targets. No compensation expense has been recognized as performance conditions are improbable.

children.title: Fair Value Determination
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 14 - Share-based Compensation/Fair Value Determination
children.summary: The fair value and derived service period of the SpaceX CEO Award and AI CEO Award are estimated on the grant date using a Monte Carlo simulation model. The weighted-average assumptions that were used to calculate the grant date fair value of the SpaceX CEO Award and modification date fair value of the AI CEO Award are as follows: [tables/table-145 Option Pricing Inputs.html] The SpaceX CEO Award and AI CEO Award do not have a defined performance period other than Mr. Musk's continued employment through the date each milestone is achieved. Therefore, an analysis was performed for an expected term of ten to fifty years and a midpoint of thirty years was used. The Company determined the expected volatility assumption using the frequency of daily historical prices of comparable public companies' common stock for a period equal to the expected term. The risk-free interest rate assumption is based upon observed interest rates on U.S. Government securities for a period consistent with the expected term. The dividend yield assumption is based on the Company's history and expectation of dividend payouts. The Company has never declared or paid any cash dividends on its Common Stock and does not anticipate paying any cash dividends in the foreseeable future.

children.title: Summary of Share-Based Compensation Information
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 14 - Share-based Compensation/Summary of Share-Based Compensation Information
children.summary: The following table summarizes our share-based compensation expense by line item in the consolidated statements of operations: [tables/table-146 Cost Breakdown.html] During the three months ended March 31, 2026 and 2025, share-based compensation expense capitalized to the consolidated balance sheets was \$60 million and \$30 million, respectively.

children.title: Note 15 - Income Taxes
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 15 - Income Taxes
children.summary: The Company's effective tax rate was $(0.1)\%$ for the three months ended March 31, 2026, compared to $(2.7)\%$ for the three months ended March 31, 2025. The change in the Company's effective tax rate was primarily due to the changes in the mix of its jurisdictional earnings. The Company's effective tax rates for the three months ended March 31, 2026 and 2025 as compared to the U.S. federal statutory rate of $21.0\%$ were primarily impacted by the mix of its jurisdictional earnings subject to different tax rates and the valuation allowances on its deferred tax assets. In assessing the realizability of deferred tax assets, the Company considered whether it is more likely than not that some or all of its net deferred tax assets will not be realizable based on the relevant weight of all positive and negative evidence. As of March 31, 2026, the Company continues to maintain a full valuation allowance against its deferred tax assets in the United States, with the exception of certain state deferred tax assets and transferrable investment tax credits that are expected to be realizable. The Company has also recorded valuation allowances in certain foreign jurisdictions where it concluded that it is more likely than not that the deferred tax assets will not be realized. The Company will continue to assess the realizability of its deferred tax assets in future periods and will adjust the valuation allowance as necessary based on changes in facts and circumstances.

children.title: Note 16 - Commitments and Contingencies
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 16 - Commitments and Contingencies

children.title: Unconditional Obligations
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 16 - Commitments and Contingencies/Unconditional Obligations
children.summary: During the three months ended March 31, 2026, there have been no material changes to the Company's unconditional obligation since December 31, 2025 other than the execution of certain purchase agreements with an unaffiliated third party to acquire additional turbines for the AI infrastructure totaling \$925 million through 2029.

children.title: Letters of Credit and Surety Bonds
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 16 - Commitments and Contingencies/Letters of Credit and Surety Bonds
children.summary: The Company had outstanding letters of credit of \$517 million at March 31, 2026 related to various customer contracts, insurance agreements, and facility lease agreements. All of the outstanding letters of credit were collateralized by restricted cash. The Company also had surety bonds of \$447 million for self-insured workers' compensation programs and other governmental licenses at March 31, 2026.

children.title: Legal Proceedings
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 16 - Commitments and Contingencies/Legal Proceedings
children.summary: The Company faces multiple legal proceedings including a European Commission fine of EUR 120 million under the DSA, a patent infringement case with a $105 million jury award, copyright infringement claims from music publishers, GDPR class actions in the Netherlands, and lawsuits over Grok's image-generation features. The Company has recorded a $399 million accrual for probable losses.

children.title: Note 17 - Related Party Transactions
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 17 - Related Party Transactions
children.summary: The Company engaged in related party transactions with Tesla and Valor Equity Partners. It purchased $34 million of Megapack products from Tesla in Q1 2026, and had previously purchased $506 million of Megapacks and $131 million of Cybertrucks by end of 2025. A failed sale-leaseback transaction with Valor for AI hardware resulted in $1,121 million current and $7,920 million non-current debt as of March 31, 2026, with $186 million interest expense. Other related party transactions were immaterial.

children.title: Note 18 - Segments
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 18 - Segments
children.summary: Following the Mergers, the Company evaluated how to view and measure performance of the combined company and potential realignment of individual entity's historical segment structure. Following this evaluation, the Company determined that as a combined company, effective in Q1 2026, the Company's Chief Executive Officer, as the Chief Operating Decision Maker (“CODM”), organizes the Company, manages resource allocations, and measures performance among three operating and reportable segments: (i) Space, (ii) Connectivity, and (iii) AI. Prior period presentations for segments conform to the current segment reporting structure. The Company's CODM assesses performance and allocates resources to operating segments based on segment income (loss) from operations by comparing actual income (loss) from operations to historical results and previously forecasted financial information. The Company's CODM does not evaluate operating and reportable segments using asset or liability information. The following tables present information as to revenues, significant segment expenses, and income (loss) from operations by the Company's reportable segments: Three Months Ended March 31, [tables/table-147 Segment Financials.html] Three Months Ended March 31, [tables/table-148 Segment Finances.html]

children.title: Note 19 - Restructuring
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 19 - Restructuring
children.summary: In 2022, X, an indirect subsidiary of the Company (through the X Merger and subsequently, xAI Merger), initiated global employee workforce reductions, the effects of which continued into 2026. The charges and credits associated with the workforce reduction include cash severance expense and other termination benefits. Total charges (credits) of \$(11) million and \$4 million associated with the workforce reduction were recorded in Restructuring charges (credits) in the consolidated statements of operations for the three months ended March 31, 2026, and 2025, respectively. The following table is a summary of the changes in the restructuring liabilities for each period presented, included within Accrued expenses and other current liabilities and Other liabilities on the consolidated balance sheets: [tables/table-149 Restructuring Liabilities.html]

children.title: Note 20 - Subsequent Events
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 20 - Subsequent Events
children.summary: The Company has evaluated subsequent events that occurred from April 1, 2026 through May 7, 2026, which is the date the consolidated financial statements were available to be issued, and determined that there were no subsequent events or transactions that required recognition or disclosure in the consolidated financial statements, except as discussed below.

children.title: Collaboration Agreement
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 20 - Subsequent Events/Collaboration Agreement
children.summary: The Company entered into a compute agreement with Cursor to improve existing models and jointly develop AI models. Concurrently, it obtained an option to acquire Cursor for $60 billion in stock, exercisable after IPO or by September 2026. Termination could trigger $1.5 billion and $8.5 billion fees. Due diligence is ongoing.

children.title: Sale-Leaseback Transaction
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 20 - Subsequent Events/Sale-Leaseback Transaction
children.summary: On April 24, 2026, CTC entered into a five-year equipment lease agreement with Valor, a related party, for certain AI infrastructure hardware (“Valor transaction III”) for total undiscounted lease payments of \$6,587 million.

children.title: Asset Acquisition
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 20 - Subsequent Events/Asset Acquisition
children.summary: On April 30, 2026, the Company entered into an asset purchase agreement with an unaffiliated third party to purchase certain mobile gas turbines and related packages for approximately \$2,000 million (the “Turbine Acquisition”). The closing of the Turbine Acquisition is expected to occur in May 2026 and is subject to customary closing conditions. The seller has also agreed to enter into a post-closing services agreement to support the Company's turbine operations. The Turbine Acquisition will help provide power to the Company's data centers.

children.title: Cloud Services Agreement
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 20 - Subsequent Events/Cloud Services Agreement
children.summary: On May 3, 2026, the Company entered into a cloud services agreement with Anthropic PBC, an AI research and development public benefit corporation, with respect to access to compute capacity. Pursuant to this agreement, the customer has agreed to pay a monthly fee through May 2029, with capacity ramping in May 2026 at a reduced fee. The agreement may be terminated by either party upon 90 days' notice. The customer will retain ownership and intellectual property rights in its content, AI models, and related data.

children.title: Shares
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS_2/Note 20 - Subsequent Events/Shares
children.summary: SPACEX Space Exploration Technologies Corp. Class A Common Stock PRELIMINARY PROSPECTUS , 2026 Through and including , 2026 (the 25th day after the date of this prospectus), all dealers effecting transactions in our Class A common stock, whether or not participating in this offering, may be required to deliver a prospectus. This delivery requirement is in addition to a dealer's obligation to deliver a prospectus when acting as an underwriter and with respect to an unsold allotment or subscription.

children.title: INFORMATION NOT REQUIRED IN PROSPECTUS
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/PART II/INFORMATION NOT REQUIRED IN PROSPECTUS
children.summary: The document covers Items 13-16 of a registration statement. Item 13 lists estimated expenses of issuance and distribution, including SEC, FINRA, and stock exchange fees. Item 14 details indemnification of directors and officers under the Texas Business Organizations Code, including mandatory and permissive indemnification, advance payment of expenses, and insurance. Item 15 reports recent unregistered securities sales, including shares issued in the xAI Merger, performance-based restricted shares to Mr. Musk, and shares issued in a spectrum license purchase agreement. Item 16 references exhibits and financial statement schedules.

children.title: (b) Financial Statement Schedules
children.path: spacex-s1.pdf/PART II/(b) Financial Statement Schedules
children.summary: The text contains standard SEC registration undertakings. It states financial schedules are omitted as not applicable. The registrant undertakes to provide certificates to underwriters. It addresses indemnification for Securities Act liabilities, noting SEC's view that such indemnification is against public policy and unenforceable, except for expenses in successful defense. The registrant will submit the question to court if needed. It also includes undertakings that omitted prospectus information under Rule 430A is deemed part of the registration statement upon effectiveness, and each post-effective amendment with a prospectus is deemed a new registration for the offering.

sections.title: SIGNATURES
sections.summary: Space Exploration Technologies Corp. files a Registration Statement under the Securities Act of 1933, signed by Elon Musk as CEO and CTO on May 20, 2026. A Power of Attorney appoints Gwynne Shotwell and Bret Johnsen to sign amendments and related documents. The statement is signed by multiple persons in their capacities.

images.path: images/image-79-Aerial View of Coastal Industrial Complex.jpg
images.summary: image-79 The image displays a sprawling industrial facility situated along a coastline during twilight. A large, brightly lit warehouse or processing plant dominates the left side, connected to a vast grid of smaller structures and storage units that extend into the marshy landscape. The complex is illuminated by artificial lighting against the darkening sky and surrounding wetlands, with distant city lights visible on the horizon.

image-80 A long-exposure photograph captures a rocket launch at dusk, showing the vehicle's trajectory as a bright streak across the sky. In the foreground, a SpaceX facility is illuminated, with a large vertical rocket booster standing on display next to the building. The city lights of a town are visible in the background under a twilight sky.

image-81 The image displays a wide aerial perspective of a large industrial facility situated in an open, flat landscape. In the foreground, there is a modern building with a parking lot and illuminated grounds. The background features numerous other structures, including tall towers and processing units, all lit up against the evening sky.

image-82 A modern commercial building featuring a dark blue and white exterior with the Starlink logo prominently displayed on the facade. The structure includes multiple rows of windows and is situated next to a parking lot, surrounded by tall evergreen trees under bright sunlight.

image-83 A contemporary building with a sharp, angular roofline is captured at twilight. The structure features extensive glass walls that reflect the deep blue sky and clouds above. Inside, warm red lighting illuminates the interior space, creating a striking contrast against the cool exterior tones.

image-84 A large rocket is shown ascending from a launchpad, leaving behind a massive cloud of smoke and fire. To the right stands a tall water tower structure, while to the left is a metallic gantry or service tower. The scene takes place near a body of water with land visible in the background under a clear sky.

image-85 Two rocket boosters are shown descending simultaneously towards a coastal launch site. One booster is hovering just above the ground with its landing legs deployed, while the second booster is slightly higher in the air, both utilizing retro-propulsion to slow their descent for a controlled vertical landing on circular pads near the ocean.

image-86 A large rocket ascends vertically into the sky, leaving a trail of smoke and fire as it lifts off from a launchpad. The scene is set against a backdrop of a clear blue sky transitioning to sunset hues near the horizon, with industrial structures visible at the base of the launch site.

image-87 The image displays a large industrial facility, likely a warehouse or distribution center, situated in a rural setting surrounded by green fields. The main building features a vast roof equipped with numerous ventilation units and solar panels. Extensive loading docks line the perimeter, accommodating many parked trucks, while adjacent areas include parking lots for vehicles and storage yards containing construction materials and equipment.

image-88 The image displays a sleek, contemporary reception area featuring a black desk with illuminated branding on the wall behind it. To the left, glass doors and a display case containing metallic figures are visible, while polished floors reflect the modern lighting fixtures above.

image-89 Aerial view of a large, autonomous drone ship floating on the ocean. The vessel features a flat deck with a central landing zone marked by concentric circles and a tall vertical mast structure rising from the middle.

image-90 A rocket ascends vertically into a clear blue sky, leaving a trail of bright exhaust and smoke. At the base, large plumes of white vapor and dust billow outward from the launch pad, which is surrounded by support towers and equipment near a coastline.

image-91 A passenger is shown holding a smartphone horizontally to watch a movie, while the seatback screen in front displays a grid of other available film options. The scene is illuminated by blue ambient lighting typical of an airplane cabin at night.

image-92 Aerial view of a large cruise liner sailing on deep blue ocean waters. The vessel features multiple decks with colorful recreational facilities, swimming pools, and water slides visible on the upper levels. Logos for Royal Caribbean Group, Celebrity Cruises, and Silversea are displayed at the bottom of the image.

image-93 The image displays the top surface of a green agricultural machine, likely part of an autonomous or smart farming system. Visible components include various antennas, wiring, and a white panel, indicating advanced technology integration for navigation or data transmission. The John Deere logo is prominently displayed in the corner.
image-94 <details> JOHN DEERE0

image-95 A large white cargo aircraft is parked on an airport tarmac under a cloudy sky. The side of the fuselage features the prominent "flexport" logo in black text, while the same branding appears as a watermark in the bottom left corner.

image-96 Aerial view of a coastal town devastated by a hurricane, with widespread destruction of buildings and infrastructure. A large white building stands partially intact amidst piles of debris and collapsed structures. Trees are stripped bare, and the landscape is littered with wreckage from homes and other facilities.
image-97 夜晚的湿滑路面上，一排车辆正在排队接受检查。画面左侧可见一辆大型车辆的侧面，其车身带有反光条纹。前方有多辆亮着红色警示灯的车辆，疑似警车或执法车。道路右侧放置了橙色的交通锥筒以引导车流。整体场景光线昏暗，主要依靠车灯和警示灯照明。

tables.summary: table-1 The table shows per share and total amounts for initial public offering price, underwriting discounts, and proceeds to Space Exploration Technologies Corp.

table-2 The table lists various categories with their corresponding values in dollars, including Space-Enabled Solutions ($370), Starlink Broadband ($870), Starlink Mobile ($740), AI Infrastructure ($2.4), Consumer Subscriptions ($760), Digital Advertising ($600), Enterprise Applications ($22.7), and Total Addressable Market ($28.5).

table-3 Space Exploration Technologies Corp. plans an IPO of Class A common stock on Nasdaq under symbol SPCX. Elon Musk will retain majority voting control. Proceeds fund AI compute, launch infrastructure, and satellite expansion.
table-4 The table shows revenue, costs, net income, and EPS for three-month and annual periods ending March 31, 2026/2025 and December 31, 2025/2024/2023, with losses in most periods.

table-5 The table shows pro forma net loss per share of common stock for three months ended March 31, 2026 ($0.41) and year ended December 31, 2025 ($0.51), with adjustments for preferred stock conversion.
table-6 The table shows net cash from operating, investing, and financing activities for three months and years ended March 31 and December 31, 2023-2026.
table-7 The table shows capital expenditures for Space, Connectivity, and AI segments for three months and years ended March 31 and December 31, with AI spending surging to $7,723M in Q1 2026.
table-8 The table shows unaudited financial data for March 31, 2026 and 2025, and December 31, 2024, including cash, total assets, liabilities, preferred stock, and shareholders' equity.
table-9 The table shows SpaceX's launches and mass to orbit for 2023-2026, with segment income and Adjusted EBITDA. Launches grew from 98 to 170, while segment income fluctuated.
table-10 Starlink subscribers grew from 5.0M to 10.3M in Q1 2026, while ARPU fell from $86 to $66. Segment income and Adjusted EBITDA increased year-over-year.
table-11 The table shows segment loss from operations and Adjusted EBITDA for three months and years ended March 31 and December 31, with nameplate compute draw in gigawatts.

table-12 The table shows financial data as of March 31, 2026, including cash, long-term debt of $29,111 million, redeemable convertible preferred stock, and shareholders' equity with multiple common stock classes.
table-13 The table shows shareholders' equity of $34,533 and total capitalization of $70,693, with additional paid-in capital of $74,083 and accumulated deficit of $41,311.
table-14 The table shows shares acquired and total consideration for Elon Musk, existing investors, new investors, and total, with percentages and average price per share.

table-15 The table lists SpaceX's key milestones from 2002 to 2026, including founding, first private orbital launch, ISS docking, rocket landing, Starship tests, Starlink growth, and subscriber targets.
table-16 The table shows yearly values from 2023 to 2025, increasing from 1,210 to 2,213.
table-17 The table shows two quarterly values: Q1 2025 with 450 and Q1 2026 with 556.

table-25 The table shows two quarterly values: 0.3 for Q1 2025 and 1.0 for Q1 2026, indicating an increase.

table-26 The table shows capital expenditures for Space, Connectivity, and AI segments for Q1 2026/2025 and full years 2025-2023. Total spending rose from $4,140M to $10,107M in Q1, driven by AI growth.
table-27 The table shows revenue percentages for Launch Services and Launch & Development segments for three-month and annual periods ending in 2023-2026.
table-28 Revenue rose 15.4% to $4,694M, but net loss widened to $4,276M from $528M, driven by a 125.7% surge in R&D costs and large other expenses.
table-29 Revenue fell 28.4% to $619M, while total costs rose 37% to $1,281M, driven by a 76.8% R&D increase, leading to a $662M operating loss.
table-30 Revenue increased 31.6% to $3,257M, costs rose 43.5% to $2,069M, and income from operations grew 15.0% to $1,188M for the three months ended March 31, 2026 vs 2025.
table-31 Revenue increased 12.5% to $818M, but total costs surged 97.7% to $3.287B, driven by a 162% rise in R&D, leading to a 163.8% wider operating loss of $2.469B.
table-32 Revenue increased 33.2% to $18,674 million in 2025, but total costs rose 56.9%, leading to a net loss of $4,937 million compared to net income of $791 million in 2024.
table-33 Revenue increased 7.6% to $4,086M in 2025, but total costs rose 25.7%, leading to an operating loss of $657M vs. $21M income in 2024.
table-34 Revenue increased 49.8% to $11,387M in 2025 from $7,599M in 2024. Total costs rose 24.5% to $6,964M. Income from operations surged 120.4% to $4,423M.
table-35 Revenue increased 22.2% to $3,201M in 2025, but total costs surged 128.6% to $9,556M, leading to a loss from operations of $6,355M, a 307.1% increase.
table-36 The table shows 2024 vs 2023 financial data: Revenue increased 34.9% to $14,015M, total costs decreased 2.5%, and net income improved from a $4,628M loss to $791M profit.
table-37 Revenue increased 6.7% to $3,796 million in 2024. Total costs rose 6.1%. Income from operations turned positive at $21 million, compared to a loss of $1 million in 2023.
table-38 Revenue increased 96.4% to $7,599M in 2024 from $3,869M in 2023. Total costs rose 64.5% to $5,593M, driving income from operations up 327.4% to $2,006M.

table-39 The table shows a decrease in revenue from $2,961M in 2023 to $2,620M in 2024, with total costs and expenses dropping significantly due to a large impairment in 2023, resulting in a reduced loss from operations of $1,561M in 2024.
table-40 The table shows net loss, adjustments (depreciation, compensation, impairments, interest, taxes), and Adjusted EBITDA for three months and years ended March 31 and December 31, 2023-2026.

table-48 The table lists various categories with their corresponding values in dollars, including Space-Enabled Solutions ($370), Starlink Broadband ($870), Starlink Mobile ($740), AI Infrastructure ($2.4), Consumer Subscriptions ($760), Digital Advertising ($600), Enterprise Applications ($22.7), and Total Addressable Market ($28.5).
table-49 Table compares SpaceX rockets: Falcon 9, Falcon Heavy, and Starship, detailing height, mass, payload capacities to LEO, GTO, and Mars, reusability, flight counts, and first launch dates.

table-50 The Falcon 9 rocket stands 70 m tall, has a 3.7 m diameter, mass of 594,054 kg, and payload capacities of 22,800 kg to LEO, 8,300 kg to GTO, and 4,020 kg to Mars. First launched June 4, 2010.
table-51 The Falcon Heavy rocket stands 70 m tall, weighs 1,420,788 kg, and can carry 63,800 kg to LEO, 26,700 kg to GTO, and 16,800 kg to Mars. Its first launch was on February 6, 2018.
table-52 The table lists SpaceX Starship's height (124.4 m), diameter (9 m), mass (5,533,000 kg), payload capacities to LEO, GTO, and Mars (all 100+ Mt), and first launch on April 20, 2023.

table-53 The Dragon spacecraft has a height of 8.1 m, diameter of 4 m, spacecraft volume of 9.3 m³, trunk volume of 37 m³, launch payload mass of 6,000 kg, return payload mass of 3,000 kg, and first flight on December 8, 2010.
table-54 344 aircraft equipped with Starlink, serving over 7 million passengers on 167,000 flights, with 3.7 million+ connected devices and double the Wi-Fi customer satisfaction scores.
table-55 Starlink offers high-bandwidth experiences, speeds up to 250 Mbps, low latency, global coverage, and faster installation for aviation and remote areas.
table-56 The table lists SpaceX executives and directors including Elon Musk (CEO, CTO, Chairman), Gwynne Shotwell (President, COO), Bret Johnsen (CFO), and several directors.
